content.body: This catalog names default categories that should eventually become reusable governed App Behavior defaults. It is not the final exhaustive rule set; it defines the starting categories, when the reusable default is enough, and when route/module overrides are required.

content.title: Not Defaultable
content.body: Defaults do not cover DTO/query shape, state ownership, command semantics, permissions, persistence, side effects, security/privacy boundaries, runtime drift, backend internals, or backend/API authority. Those require runtime/source/API/test discovery, backend authority references, explicit App Behavior rows, or true product decisions.

content.title: Loading
body: Intent:
Acknowledge that work is in progress and prevent users from mistaking delay for failure.
Reusable default is enough when:
The operation is ordinary, low-risk, and has no special stale, permission, token, upload, or external-side-effect behavior.
Override is required when:
Loading affects command admission, stale data, identity, privacy, public token state, uploads/downloads, long-running work, partial hydration, or proof readiness.

content.title: Empty State
body: Intent:
Explain that no usable results or records are available and preserve the next meaningful action.
Reusable default is enough when:
The empty state has no product-specific cause, no permission ambiguity, and no special recovery path.
Override is required when:
Empty can mean filtered results, missing permissions, incomplete profile, unavailable source, deleted object, expired token, or a meaningful product milestone.

content.title: Recoverable Error
body: Intent:
Make failure understandable and give users a safe way to retry, adjust input, return, or continue.
Reusable default is enough when:
The error is generic, non-destructive, and does not expose private details or require product-specific recovery.
Override is required when:
Failure involves tokens, payments, external sends, uploads/downloads, partial success, permission, privacy, compliance, data loss, or user support escalation.

content.title: Unauthorized Or Forbidden
body: Intent:
Protect private behavior while explaining the user's safe next step.
Reusable default is enough when:
The access state is ordinary and governed by a shared auth/role pattern.
Override is required when:
The route is public-token, admin, delegated, impersonated, identity-switching, or uses stored intent/post-login resume.

content.title: Disabled While Submitting
body: Intent:
Prevent duplicate submission and make in-flight command state clear.
Reusable default is enough when:
The command is single-step, idempotent enough for ordinary retry, and has no special cancellation or partial-success behavior.
Override is required when:
The command has external side effects, file upload, irreversible state, duplicate semantics, optimistic updates, or cross-owner projection updates.

content.title: Required-Field Validation Display
body: Intent:
Show what blocks progress and how the user can fix it.
Reusable default is enough when:
Required fields are ordinary, local to the form, and have predictable labels and recovery.
Override is required when:
Requirements are conditional, derived, server-owned, role-dependent, upload-dependent, cross-field, exact-copy governed, or tied to legal/privacy meaning.

content.title: Stale Data Indication
body: Intent:
Prevent users from trusting outdated information when freshness changes meaning.
Reusable default is enough when:
A simple refresh or background reload resolves the state without data risk.
Override is required when:
Staleness affects command admission, list/detail consistency, parent state patching, concurrent edits, permissions, public tokens, or financial/privacy/safety meaning.

content.title: Retry
body: Intent:
Let users recover from transient failures without losing work or duplicating effects.
Reusable default is enough when:
Retry is safe, local, and does not duplicate side effects.
Override is required when:
Retry can resend email, duplicate a mutation, re-upload a file, charge/create/update records, replay a token, or conflict with stale state.

content.title: Cancel Or Close Without Persistence
body: Intent:
Let users leave temporary UI without surprise saved-state changes or work loss.
Reusable default is enough when:
The UI is read-only or contains only ephemeral, safely discardable state.
Override is required when:
The UI has drafts, dirty state, uploaded files, multi-step progress, external effects, pending commands, or stored intent.

content.title: Responsive Containment
body: Intent:
Keep content readable, reachable, and non-overlapping across supported viewport sizes.
Reusable default is enough when:
The layout uses ordinary stacking, wrapping, scrolling, or table/card conversion without changing behavior meaning.
Override is required when:
Breakpoint behavior changes available actions, information hierarchy, table/card semantics, sticky controls, map/file preview, modals, drawers, or accessibility behavior.

content.title: Focus And Accessibility Baseline
body: Intent:
Keep controls reachable, labeled, and understandable for keyboard and assistive technology users.
Reusable default is enough when:
Controls, headings, form fields, dialogs, and status messages follow standard accessible patterns.
Override is required when:

Behavior includes custom widgets, drag/drop, complex tables, modals/drawers, toasts that carry required meaning, live async status, keyboard shortcuts, canvas/map/file previews, or exact announcement behavior.
